
# Set up

Import packages
```{r}
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  suppressWarnings({
  library(tidyverse)   # Data wrangling
  library(here)        # File management
  library(readxl)      # Read excel files
  })})
```

Set settings and universal variables
```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = here())

options(scipen = 999)
```

# Crosswalk data

These are the drinking water demand units in CalSim we want to focus on
```{r}
crosswalk = read.csv("Outputs/Crosswalk demand and delivery IDs/20260409_crosswalk_processed.csv")
```

# Demand data
```{r}
static_demand = read.csv("Outputs/Static demand/20260413_static_demand_by_du_updated.csv")%>%
  rename(dwuc = traced_du, annual_demand = demand_du_taf)%>%
  filter(dwuc %in% crosswalk$dwuc)%>%
  crossing(year = 1921:2021)%>%
  mutate(annual_demand = case_when(
    year == 1921 ~ annual_demand*0.25,
    year == 2021 ~ annual_demand*0.75,
    T ~ annual_demand))
```



# Delivery data

Demand and delivery data downloaded from the respective Google Drive folder linked to each scenario in [this spreadsheet](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1iXqEc4sMVC8gKqPzhf_VOS32EXKzocAP2pNJ5PZlFbY/edit?gid=0#gid=0). Once in a scenario's folder, click on 'Data Extraction', then 'Demands_Deliveries'. We want to download csvs of monthly values for both demand and deliveries.

Function to extract scenario ID from filename
```{r}
extract_scenario_id = function(filename) {
  str_extract(filename, "s\\d+") %>% tolower()}
```

# Processing functions

## Process individual file

Core function to process either a delivery file. 

Cleans up header rows, extracts values for delivery (in TAF), and pivots longer so we have 1 row per demand unit per month (rather than 1 column per demand unit, which is how the raw data is structured). Filters for only demand units whose delivery ID is in our crosswalk dataset

Returns a processed dataset of delivery values by demand unit and month
```{r}
process_deliveries = function(file_path, crosswalk) {
  scenario_id = extract_scenario_id(basename(file_path))
  
  # Read and clean the data
  data = read.csv(file_path) %>%
    mutate(row_id = row_number())%>%
    pivot_longer(cols = -row_id,
                 names_to = "col_position",
                 values_to = "value")%>%
    pivot_wider(
      names_from = row_id,
      values_from = value)%>%
    rename(
      col_position = 1,
      delivery_id = 2,    
      units = 7)
  
  dates = data%>%
    slice(1) %>%  
    select(8:ncol(.)) %>%
    pivot_longer(
      cols = everything(),
      names_to = "date_id",
      values_to = "date")
  
  processed = data%>%
    filter(units == "TAF" & delivery_id %in% crosswalk$delivery_id)%>%
    select(-c(1,3:units))%>%
    pivot_longer(
      cols = -delivery_id,
      names_to = "date_id",
      values_to = "delivery_taf")%>%
    left_join(dates, by = "date_id")%>%
    mutate(scenario_id = scenario_id,
           delivery_taf = as.numeric(ifelse(delivery_taf == "", NA, delivery_taf)),
           year = year(date))%>%
    select(scenario_id, delivery_id, date, year, delivery_taf) %>%
    left_join(crosswalk%>%select(dwuc, delivery_id), by = "delivery_id")
  
  return(processed)}
```

## Process complete scenario

Main function to process a complete scenario (demand + delivery pair):

Goes through the demand and delivery folders to identify scenarios, and if both files found for a given scenario, calls the function above to process both the demand and delivery data. 

Creates a dataset of missing demand units for that scenario and writes to a csv for each scenario.

Joins demand and delivery data by demand unit ID and filters out rows with any missing values. Calculates monthly % of demand met and writes to a csv

Aggregates demand and delivery by year for each demand unit and calculates annual % of demand met. Saves this to a csv for each scenario as well

Calculates tiers based on annual % of demand met
```{r}
process_scenario = function(scenario_id, delivery_folder, crosswalk, output_base = "Outputs") {
  cat("Processing scenario:", scenario_id, "\n")
  
  # Find files for this scenario
  delivery_files = list.files(delivery_folder, pattern = paste0(scenario_id, ".*\\.csv$"), full.names = TRUE)
  
  # Process files
  monthly_deliveries = process_deliveries(delivery_files[1], crosswalk)
  
  # Save monthly data
  monthly_dir = file.path(output_base, "Monthly deliveries")
  dir.create(monthly_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)
  write.csv(monthly_deliveries, file.path(monthly_dir, paste0(scenario_id, "_monthly_deliveries.csv")), row.names = FALSE)
  
  # Aggregate deliveries by year
  annual_deliveries = monthly_deliveries %>%
    group_by(scenario_id, year, dwuc) %>%
    reframe(annual_delivery = sum(delivery_taf)) 
  
  # Combine with annual demands 
  combined_annual = full_join(annual_deliveries, static_demand, by = c("dwuc", "year"))%>%
    mutate(percent_demand_met = ifelse(annual_delivery >= annual_demand, 100, annual_delivery / annual_demand * 100))%>%
    select(scenario_id, dwuc, year, annual_delivery, annual_demand, percent_demand_met)
  
  # Save annual data 
  annual_dir = file.path(output_base, "Annual demand met")
  dir.create(annual_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)
  write.csv(combined_annual, file.path(annual_dir, paste0(scenario_id, "_demand_met_by_year.csv")), row.names = FALSE)
  
  # Calculate tiers
  tiers = combined_annual %>%
    mutate(percent_demand_met = round(percent_demand_met),
      demand_met = ifelse(percent_demand_met >= 90, 1, 0)) %>%
    group_by(scenario_id, dwuc) %>%
    reframe(
      perc_years_met = mean(demand_met, na.rm = T) * 100,
      min_met = min(percent_demand_met),
      below_50 = mean(percent_demand_met < 50)*100)%>%
    ungroup() %>%
    mutate(
      tier = case_when(
        perc_years_met >= 90 & min_met > 70 ~ 1,
        perc_years_met >= 50 & min_met > 50 ~ 2,
        perc_years_met >= 50 & below_50 <= 20 ~ 3,
        T ~ 4)) %>%
    select(scenario_id, dwuc, tier, perc_years_met)
  
  return(tiers)}
```



## Process all scenarios

Last function to go through the demand and delivery folders, pull out all the files, and calls upon the function above to process each scenario and generate the corresponding outputs. 

Extracts the tier categorisations for each scenario and combines it into one matrix containing tier values across all scenarios and demand units, following the example in [this folder](https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1PMgwXjWK0eHX_2Zac4_QNrA_BaFZNpF0). 
Saves the master tier matrix as a csv
```{r}
# Process ALL scenarios and create tier matrix
process_all_scenarios = function(delivery_folder, crosswalk, output_base = "Outputs") {
  
  # Get scenario IDs
  delivery_files = list.files(delivery_folder, pattern = "\\.csv$")
  delivery_scenarios = sapply(delivery_files, extract_scenario_id)
  
  cat("Found", length(delivery_scenarios), "scenarios to process\n\n")
  
  # Process each scenario and collect results
  all_tiers = bind_rows(map(delivery_scenarios, function(scenario_id) {
    process_scenario(scenario_id, delivery_folder, crosswalk, output_base)}))%>%
    filter(!is.na(scenario_id))
  
  # Convert to matrix format for both categorical and continuous tier results
  tier_matrix_categorical = all_tiers %>%
    select(-perc_years_met)%>%
    pivot_wider(
      names_from = dwuc,
      values_from = tier,
      names_sort = T)%>%
    arrange(scenario_id)
  
  tier_matrix_continuous = all_tiers %>%
    select(-tier)%>%
    pivot_wider(
      names_from = dwuc,
      values_from = perc_years_met,
      names_sort = T)%>%
    arrange(scenario_id)
  
  # Save tier outputs
  tier_dir = file.path(output_base, "Tier summaries")
  dir.create(tier_dir, showWarnings = F, recursive = T)
  write.csv(tier_matrix_categorical, file.path(tier_dir, "20260413_all_scenarios_through_cc95_tiers_categorical.csv"), row.names = F)
  write.csv(tier_matrix_continuous, file.path(tier_dir, "20260413_all_scenarios_through_cc95_tiers_continuous.csv"), row.names = F)}
```

# Run functions

Specify demand and delivery folders as inputs, as well as crosswalk sheet with key to connect demand unit ID, demand-specific ID, and delivery-specific ID. 
Specify folder where outputs will be saved
```{r}
results = process_all_scenarios(
  delivery_folder = "Inputs/Deliveries",
  crosswalk = crosswalk,
  output_base = "Outputs")
```

Plot for baseline
```{r}
read.csv("Outputs/Tier summaries/20260413_all_scenarios_through_cc95_tiers_categorical.csv")%>%
  filter(scenario_id == "s0020")%>%
  pivot_longer(-scenario_id, names_to = "du", values_to = "tier")%>%
  filter(!is.na(tier))%>%
  group_by(scenario_id)%>%
  mutate(total_n = n())%>%
  ungroup()%>%
  group_by(scenario_id, tier)%>%
  reframe(prop = n()/total_n)%>%
  unique()%>%
  ggplot(aes(tier, prop, fill = as.factor(tier)))+
  geom_col(position="dodge")+
  labs(x= "Tier", y = "Proportion of Demand Units", fill = "Tier")+
  theme_bw()+
  theme(legend.position = "None")
```

Check to make sure nothing looks off (using baseline as example)
```{r}
read.csv("Outputs/Annual demand met/s0020_demand_met_by_year.csv")%>%
  arrange(dwuc)%>%
  filter(percent_demand_met < 70)
```
